criteria.criterion: A `_diff_links_inbound` unit test on the CAPTURED LIVE REB-458 issuelinks JSON (outwardIssue Blocks) asserts relation == `blocks`; on REB-461 JSON (inwardIssue Blocks) asserts relation == `depends_on`. RED on current code. Proving command: `pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/ -k link_direction -q`.
A `_diff_links_inbound` test with the counterpart owning the inverse edge asserts 0 emitted mutations (inverse-aware dedup). RED on a pure-swap fix.
The removal-path mirror (`_diff_link_removals`) has an equivalent absolute-direction test. RED on current code.
Existing tests that encode the reversed convention (test_reconcile_roundtrip.py:522, test_link_dedup.py:198) are corrected to the live-Jira shape and pass.

citation.description: Ticket comment (2026-07-17, Joe Oakhart): 'ran the fixed _diff_links_inbound against real REB-project issuelinks for 54 affected tickets with the active local map -> 0 emits (0 cycle-rejections, 0 mirror-adds; converged). Absolute-direction unit tests pinned to captured live-Jira fixtures (REB-458/461) pass; 1287 reconciler unit tests + full default suite green. Landed on main as e6f3283a0.' Commit e6f3283a0 named explicitly.

criteria.criterion: [operator-attested] LIVE fix-verification (pre-land): the fixed inbound differ, run against real REB issuelinks for the ~815 affected pairs, emits 0 cycle-rejecting mutations and 0 mirror-adds.
`make test` passes; no new external dependency.
Fix BOTH mirror sites in lockstep (inbound add AND removal path). [chronic-pattern guardrail]
UNIFY the duplicated direction logic into ONE helper (`_JIRA_LINK_TO_RELATION` re-declared in acli_graph.py:53, inbound_differ.py:459 — one source of truth). [chronic-pattern guardrail]
The dedup map MUST include the FULL local set (incl. archived tickets) — cf10 owns the inverse edge; the fix must not re-emit mirrors to archived counterparts.
